Petition To Determine Ownership Of Estate Assets; For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Estate Property To The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Continued To Jan-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica & Arif Virji for (petitioner) Anthony Abuzeide. Michelle Domingo for (respondent) Taylor Henley. Daniel Bernhard specially appearing for Open Road. David Eisenmann (via court call) for CNC Motors.

The Court asked who is claiming ownership of the Porsche and Ms. Zulaica responded that the Estate is. Mr. Bernhard stated that Open Road owns the Porsche. Ms. Zulaica stated that they are currently conducting discovery, that Open Road is trying to file an objection and that they would like 45 days to finish discovery, that there was an injunction to sell the Porsche but that it is currently in Open Road's possession. Mr. Bernhard stated that they plan to sell the Porsche, that they have title, that the action here should be stayed as everything about the car has taken place in Washington. Ms. Zulaica reported that the car was taken to Canada without authority, that they are not sure if the car is in Washington and that most actions took place in California. Ms. Domingo stated that they have responded to the amended petition and are now in discovery. Mr. Eisenmann stated that CNC Motors is the second dealer to purchase the car and that they sold it to the third dealer. Mr. Bernhard stated that the third dealer sold the car to Open Road. Ms. Zulaica stated that the car has been sold and that there are intermediaries. Mr. Bernhard stated that they are proceeding in Washington and that they need a hearing date to file the petition. Mr. Virji stated that there is one other party, a dealership in Burlingame, that is a potential new party. The Petition to determine Ownership of Estate Assets, etc. was continued to January 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not Reported)

Petition For An Order Approving Indemnification And Hold Harmless Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica and Evan Winet for Anthony Abuzeide (Petitioner).
Thomas McDermott appeared by telephone for Woodrow Monte.

Mr. McDermott informed the Court that he is not licensed to practice in California, that he is not making an appearance and that he is on the line to address any concerns or questions the Court may have. Ms. Zulaica stated that she is not aware of any objections, that the Beneficiaries were noticed and have been kept up to date, that the total to be paid to Mr. McDermott is $5,300.00 and that she is fine with the specific amount being reflected in the order. Ms. Zulaica also stated that she spoke with her Client this morning, that she believes all of the guns listed (1 through 11) have been turned over to the Oregon Sheriff's Department, that there are probably 100 or more guns, that the Executor is going to Oregon to sift through them, and that some of the guns are with the San Francisco Police Department and some are with the Sheriff's in Oregon. In addition, Ms. Zulaica stated that the idea is to have James Julia (Gun Dealer) pick the guns up and help with selling them, that she is waiting to get the inventory and appraisal back from the Referee and that it will be a partial inventory. The Court granted the Petition for an Order Approving Indemnification and Hold Harmless Agreement (subject to the order being reviewed and revised).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
